sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.33 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Suppose that all$2$-gene\-ra\-tor subgroups of a finite$2$-group$G$are metacyclic. Is the derived length of$G$bounded? This is true for finite$p$-groups if$p 2$, see 1.1.8 in (M.\,Suzuki, Structure of a group and the structure of its lattice of subgroups, Springer, Berlin, 1956). { Ya.\,G.\,Berkovich} Yes, it is at most 2 (E.\,Crestani, F.\,Menegazzo, , { 15}, no.\,3 (2012), 359--383).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
